arabic: نعمتان مغبون فيهما كثير من الناس الصحة والفراغ
english: There are two blessings which many people lose: health and free time.
lesson: Health and free time are blessings people often fail to value until they are gone.
life_application: Schedule your free hours toward worship, learning, or productive work instead of letting them pass unused.

arabic: اغتنم خمسا قبل خمس شبابك قبل هرمك وصحتك قبل سقمك وغناك قبل فقرك وفراغك قبل شغلك وحياتك قبل موتك
english: Take advantage of five before five: your youth before your old age, your health before your illness, your wealth before your poverty, your free time before your preoccupation, and your life before your death.
lesson: Every stage of ease is temporary and must be used before it is replaced by limitation.
life_application: List the resources you currently have and set concrete goals to use each of them before circumstances change.

arabic: لا تزول قدما ابن ادم يوم القيامة حتى يسال عن عمره فيم افناه وعن علمه فيم فعل فيه وعن ماله من اين اكتسبه وفيم انفقه وعن جسمه فيم ابلاه
english: The feet of the son of Adam will not move on the Day of Resurrection until he is asked about his life and how he spent it, his knowledge and what he did with it, his wealth, how he earned it and how he spent it, and his body, how he wore it out.
lesson: A person is held directly accountable for how they spent their lifetime, knowledge, wealth, and body.
life_application: Periodically review your daily schedule and ask whether you could justify how you spent it if questioned today.

arabic: اذا امسيت فلا تنتظر الصباح واذا اصبحت فلا تنتظر المساء وخذ من صحتك لمرضك ومن حياتك لموتك
english: When you reach the evening, do not wait for the morning, and when you reach the morning, do not wait for the evening. Take benefit from your health before your illness, and from your life before your death.
lesson: Good deeds and important matters should be attended to immediately rather than postponed.
life_application: If a task can be done today, do it today instead of assuming tomorrow is guaranteed.

arabic: بادروا بالاعمال فتنا كقطع الليل المظلم
english: Hasten to do good deeds before there come trials like pieces of a dark night.
lesson: Delaying good actions is risky, since unforeseen circumstances may soon make them impossible to do.
life_application: Do not delay important good deeds, since future opportunity, health, and time are never guaranteed.

arabic: يهرم ابن ادم وتشب منه اثنتان الحرص على المال والحرص على العمر
english: The son of Adam grows old, but two things in him remain young: love of wealth and love of long life.
lesson: People naturally assume they still have plenty of time left, which fuels wasted time and procrastination.
life_application: Recognize the tendency to assume unlimited time remains, and counter it by prioritizing important tasks now.

arabic: احب الاعمال الى الله ادومها وان قل
english: The most beloved of deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if they are small.
lesson: Consistency in small good deeds over time is more valuable than occasional bursts of effort.
life_application: Choose small, manageable daily habits you can sustain rather than large efforts you cannot maintain.

arabic: اللهم اني اعوذ بك من العجز والكسل
english: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from incapacity and laziness.
lesson: The Prophet ﷺ regularly sought Allah's protection from the very traits that waste time and effort.
life_application: Make this supplication part of your daily routine, especially before starting important tasks.

arabic: ما اكل احد طعاما قط خيرا من ان ياكل من عمل يده
english: No one has ever eaten food better than that which he eats from the work of his own hand.
lesson: Using one's time productively through honest, personal effort is dignified and praiseworthy.
life_application: Value the time you invest in honest work as an act of worship, not merely a worldly necessity.

arabic: المؤمن القوي خير واحب الى الله من المؤمن الضعيف وفي كل خير احرص على ما ينفعك واستعن بالله ولا تعجز
english: The strong believer is better and more beloved to Allah than the weak believer, though there is good in both. Be eager for what benefits you, seek help from Allah, and do not become helpless.
lesson: A believer should actively pursue what benefits them with energy and purpose rather than wasting time in passivity.
life_application: When facing a task, combine practical effort with reliance on Allah instead of procrastinating or giving up.

arabic: وشاب نشا في عبادة الله
english: ...and a young man who grew up devoted to the worship of Allah...
lesson: Spending one's youth in worship and good deeds earns one of the highest honors, the shade of Allah on the Day of Resurrection.
life_application: Use the energy and free time of your youth to build strong habits of worship before old age or distraction sets in.

arabic: الصلاة على وقتها
english: [The most beloved deed to Allah is] prayer at its proper time.
lesson: Punctuality in fulfilling one's obligations, starting with prayer, is among the most beloved acts to Allah.
life_application: Treat deadlines and appointed times, especially the five daily prayers, as priorities not to be delayed without reason.

arabic: تلك صلاة المنافق يجلس يرقب الشمس حتى اذا كانت بين قرني شيطان قام فنقرها اربعا لا يذكر الله فيها الا قليلا
english: That is the prayer of the hypocrite: he sits watching the sun until it is between the two horns of Shaytan, then he gets up and pecks out four rak'ahs, barely remembering Allah in them.
lesson: Delaying an obligation to the very last moment and rushing through it carelessly is a sign of poor time discipline.
life_application: Perform your duties, especially prayer, promptly and attentively rather than delaying them until the last possible moment.

arabic: اللهم بارك لامتي في بكورها
english: O Allah, bless my nation in its early mornings.
lesson: The early part of the day carries special blessing, and starting the day early is encouraged.
life_application: Begin your day early rather than sleeping through the productive morning hours, to seek the blessing the Prophet ﷺ asked for.
source: Sunan Abi Dawud and Jami' al-Tirmidhi

arabic: من صلى الصبح فهو في ذمة الله
english: Whoever prays the Fajr prayer is under the protection of Allah.
lesson: Starting the day with the Fajr prayer places a person under Allah's special protection for the rest of the day.
life_application: Make praying Fajr on time the anchor of your daily schedule, building the rest of your productive day around it.

arabic: خيركم من طال عمره وحسن عمله
english: The best of you are those who live long and do good deeds.
lesson: A long life is only truly praiseworthy when it is filled with good deeds; time itself is not enough without purpose.
life_application: Do not just aim to live long; aim to fill each additional year with meaningful, righteous action.

arabic: من سره ان يبسط له في رزقه وينسا له في اثره فليصل رحمه
english: Whoever wishes for his provision to be expanded and his lifespan to be extended, let him maintain the ties of kinship.
lesson: Certain good deeds, like maintaining family ties, are a means through which Allah grants barakah in one's time and provision.
life_application: Invest time regularly in visiting and caring for relatives, viewing it as an investment that brings blessing to your life.

arabic: مثل الذي يذكر ربه والذي لا يذكر ربه مثل الحي والميت
english: The example of the one who remembers his Lord and the one who does not remember his Lord is like that of the living and the dead.
lesson: Filling one's time with remembrance of Allah brings the heart to life, while neglecting it leaves a person spiritually inert.
life_application: Insert short moments of dhikr throughout your daily schedule rather than letting the day pass without remembrance of Allah.

arabic: تعرض الاعمال يوم الاثنين ويوم الخميس فاحب ان يعرض عملي وانا صائم
english: Deeds are presented [to Allah] on Monday and Thursday, so I like my deeds to be presented while I am fasting.
lesson: The Prophet ﷺ structured his week deliberately around times when deeds are especially presented to Allah.
life_application: Organize your week with specific days set aside for extra good deeds or worship, rather than treating every day the same without intention.

arabic: افضل الصدقة ان تصدق وانت صحيح شحيح تخشى الفقر وتامل الغنى ولا تمهل حتى اذا بلغت الحلقوم قلت لفلان كذا ولفلان كذا وقد كان لفلان
english: The best charity is that you give while you are healthy, feeling stingy, fearing poverty and hoping for wealth. Do not delay until, when death approaches your throat, you say 'this is for so-and-so and that for so-and-so,' when it already belongs to that person by then.
lesson: Good deeds should be done while one is still capable, not delayed until it is too late to benefit from them.
life_application: Act on good intentions such as charity or reconciliation now, rather than postponing them to an uncertain future.

arabic: من نام عن الوتر او نسيه فليصله اذا ذكره او استيقظ
english: Whoever sleeps through Witr prayer or forgets it should pray it when he remembers or wakes up.
lesson: Islam provides a way to recover a missed time-bound act rather than abandoning it entirely.
life_application: If you miss a daily habit due to circumstances beyond your control, make it up as soon as possible instead of giving up on it.
source: Sunan Abi Dawud and Jami' al-Tirmidhi

arabic: من نام عن حزبه او عن شيء منه فقراه فيما بين صلاة الفجر وصلاة الظهر كتب له كانما قراه من الليل
english: Whoever sleeps and misses his portion [of night recitation], or part of it, and then recites it between Fajr and Dhuhr, it is recorded for him as if he had recited it at night.
lesson: A missed portion of a daily practice can still be recovered within a set window, preserving consistency.
life_application: Build a recovery window into your habits, such as a set time the next day, so a missed session does not derail your consistency.

arabic: ما ندمت على شيء ندامتي على يوم غربت شمسه نقص فيه اجلي ولم يزدد فيه عملي
english: I have never regretted anything as much as I have regretted a day whose sun set, in which my lifespan decreased but my good deeds did not increase.
lesson: Every day that passes without benefit is an irreversible loss, since one's remaining lifespan constantly decreases.
life_application: At the end of each day, honestly assess whether you added any good to your record, and resolve to do better the next day.

arabic: يا ابن ادم انما انت ايام فاذا ذهب يومك ذهب بعضك
english: O son of Adam, you are but a collection of days. Whenever a day passes, part of you has passed away.
lesson: A person's life is literally made up of individual days, each of which is an irreplaceable piece of the total lifespan.
life_application: Treat each day as a finite asset; review at day's end what you accomplished and what you must not repeat tomorrow.

arabic: حاسبوا انفسكم قبل ان تحاسبوا وزنوا انفسكم قبل ان توزنوا
english: Call yourselves to account before you are called to account, and weigh your deeds before they are weighed for you.
lesson: Regular self-review of how one's time and deeds are spent prepares a person for the greater accounting in the Hereafter.
life_application: Set aside a regular time each week to review how you spent your time and correct any misuse before it becomes habitual.

arabic: اني لابغض الرجل ان اراه فارغا ليس في عمل دنيا ولا اخرة
english: I truly dislike seeing a man who is idle, engaged in nothing of this world nor of the Hereafter.
lesson: A believer's time should be occupied with either productive worldly work or acts that benefit the Hereafter, not wasted in idleness.
life_application: Avoid long stretches of unproductive idleness; fill your free time with useful work, learning, or worship.
reference: Musannaf Ibn Abi Shaybah, Kitab al-Zuhd

arabic: ما من يوم يصبح العباد فيه الا ملكان ينزلان فيقول احدهما اللهم اعط منفقا خلفا ويقول الاخر اللهم اعط ممسكا تلفا
english: There is no morning in which servants rise but two angels descend, one of them saying, 'O Allah, give the one who spends a replacement,' and the other saying, 'O Allah, give the one who withholds destruction [of his wealth].'
lesson: Each new day carries its own decree, and how a person uses that day, including spending in good, affects their outcome.
life_application: Begin every morning with an intention to use the day generously and productively, since each day is a fresh, distinct opportunity.

arabic: اذا مات ابن ادم انقطع عمله الا من ثلاث صدقة جارية او علم ينتفع به او ولد صالح يدعو له
english: When the son of Adam dies, his deeds come to an end except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who prays for him.
lesson: Time invested wisely during one's life can produce benefit that continues even after death.
life_application: Use part of your time now to build something lasting, such as teaching others, raising righteous children, or establishing ongoing charity.

arabic: تعلموا قبل ان تسودوا
english: Learn [knowledge] before you assume positions of leadership.
lesson: Time should be used to prepare and build competence before taking on greater responsibility.
life_application: Use your available time now to prepare for future responsibilities rather than waiting until you are already burdened with them.
source: Mentioned in Sahih al-Bukhari (Kitab al-'Ilm); connected by al-Darimi and Ibn Abi Shaybah
reference: Sahih al-Bukhari, Book of Knowledge; Sunan al-Darimi 1/103

arabic: عجبا لامر المؤمن ان امره كله خير وليس ذلك لاحد الا للمؤمن ان اصابته سراء شكر فكان خيرا له وان اصابته ضراء صبر فكان خيرا له
english: How wonderful is the affair of the believer, for his affairs are all good, and this applies to no one but the believer: if something good happens to him, he is grateful, and that is good for him; and if harm befalls him, he is patient, and that is good for him.
lesson: A believer turns every period of time, whether pleasant or difficult, into an opportunity for reward through gratitude or patience.
life_application: In both good and hard times, respond intentionally with gratitude or patience so that no period of your life goes to waste spiritually.

arabic: من اصبح منكم امنا في سربه معافى في جسده عنده قوت يومه فكانما حيزت له الدنيا
english: Whoever among you wakes up secure in his home, healthy in his body, and has his food for the day, it is as if the whole world had been gathered for him.
lesson: Each day's basic blessings of safety, health, and provision are significant and worth recognizing and using well.
life_application: Start each day by consciously recognizing these basic blessings, which motivates gratitude and purposeful use of the day ahead.

arabic: لا تزال الملائكة تصلي على احدكم ما دام في مجلسه الذي صلى فيه تقول اللهم اغفر له اللهم ارحمه ما لم يحدث او يؤذ فيه
english: The angels continue to invoke blessings upon one of you as long as he remains in the place where he prayed, saying, 'O Allah, forgive him, O Allah, have mercy on him,' as long as he does not invalidate his ablution or cause harm there.
lesson: Time spent waiting after worship can itself become a source of continued reward if used properly.
life_application: Instead of rushing off after completing an obligation, use a few extra moments for additional remembrance or reflection to gain further reward.

arabic: ان الزمان قد استدار كهيئته يوم خلق الله السماوات والارض
english: Indeed, time has completed its cycle and returned to its original form, as it was on the day Allah created the heavens and the earth.
lesson: Time follows a fixed, divinely ordained order, reminding believers of the structured nature of months and years established by Allah.
life_application: Recognize the sacred rhythm of the Islamic calendar and plan your worship and important commitments in harmony with it.

arabic: ان الدنيا حلوة خضرة وان الله مستخلفكم فيها فينظر كيف تعملون فاتقوا الدنيا واتقوا النساء
english: Indeed, the world is sweet and attractive, and Allah has made you stewards in it to see how you will act, so be mindful regarding the world and be mindful regarding women.
lesson: A person's time in this world is a test of stewardship, and one's actions during it are being observed and will be judged.
life_application: View your time and resources as a trust from Allah to be managed responsibly, not as something to indulge carelessly.

arabic: تعجلوا الى الحج يعني الفريضة فان احدكم لا يدري ما يعرضه
english: Hasten to perform Hajj, meaning the obligatory one, for none of you knows what may prevent him [in the future].
lesson: Obligations that one is able to fulfill should not be delayed, since future ability or opportunity is never guaranteed.
life_application: When you have the means and ability to fulfill an important religious or life obligation, act on it promptly rather than postponing it.
source: Sunan Abi Dawud and Sunan Ibn Majah
reference: Sunan Abi Dawud 1732; Sunan Ibn Majah 2883

arabic: اعقلها وتوكل
english: Tie it [your camel] and [then] rely upon Allah.
lesson: Wise use of time and effort requires taking practical precautions while placing trust in Allah, not neglecting one for the other.
life_application: Plan and take reasonable practical steps toward your goals and daily tasks, then place your trust in Allah for the outcome.

arabic: لا يلدغ المؤمن من جحر واحد مرتين
english: A believer is not stung twice from the same hole.
lesson: A wise believer learns from past mistakes in how time and effort were wasted and takes precautions against repeating them.
life_application: After wasting time on something unproductive or harmful, put concrete safeguards in place so you do not repeat the same pattern.

arabic: من غدا الى المسجد لا يريد الا ان يتعلم خيرا او يعلمه كان له كاجر حاج تاما حجته
english: Whoever goes out to the mosque intending only to learn or teach some good will have a reward like that of a pilgrim who completed his pilgrimage.
lesson: Deliberately using part of one's time to learn or teach beneficial knowledge yields immense reward.
life_application: Dedicate specific, recurring time in your schedule to learning or teaching something beneficial, treating it as a high priority.

arabic: العلم خزائن ومفتاحها السؤال
english: Knowledge is treasure-troves, and the key to it is asking questions.
lesson: Time invested in seeking knowledge, including asking questions, unlocks lasting benefit for a person's life.
life_application: Do not let time pass in confusion or assumption; use it to ask and seek out correct knowledge instead.
source: Jami' Bayan al-'Ilm wa Fadlih, Ibn Abd al-Barr

arabic: اكلفوا من الاعمال ما تطيقون فان الله لا يمل حتى تملوا
english: Take on only as much of good deeds as you can sustain, for Allah does not tire [of giving reward] until you tire [of doing good].
lesson: Wise time management in worship and good deeds means pacing oneself sustainably rather than burning out quickly.
life_application: When starting a new habit or commitment, choose a manageable pace you can maintain long-term instead of overcommitting.

arabic: ان الدين يسر ولن يشاد الدين احد الا غلبه فسددوا وقاربوا وابشروا واستعينوا بالغدوة والروحة وشيء من الدلجة
english: Indeed, the religion is easy, and no one makes it a burden except that it overwhelms him. So be steadfast, come near to perfection, rejoice, and seek help through worship in the morning, the afternoon, and a portion of the night.
lesson: Spreading effort and worship across different times of the day sustains progress better than overwhelming intensity.
life_application: Distribute your important tasks or acts of worship across the day in manageable segments rather than concentrating everything into one exhausting session.

arabic: ومن يستعفف يعفه الله ومن يستغن يغنه الله ومن يصبر يصبره الله
english: Whoever abstains from asking others, Allah will make him abstain; whoever seeks self-sufficiency, Allah will make him self-sufficient; and whoever seeks patience, Allah will grant him patience.
lesson: Deliberate discipline over one's desires and time leads to real contentment and strength granted by Allah.
life_application: Practice restraint and patience in your daily choices, trusting that consistent self-discipline over time builds genuine independence.
